questions.questionText: Pelvic inflammatory disease (PID) is most commonly caused by:
questions.optionB: Neisseria gonorrhoeae and Chlamydia trachomatis (sexually transmitted ascending infections of upper genital tract)
questions.explanation: PID: infection of upper genital tract (endometritis → salpingitis → tubo-ovarian abscess → peritonitis). Most common organisms: N. gonorrhoeae and C. trachomatis (STIs — ascending from cervix). Also: anaerobes, G. vaginalis, Mycoplasma. Symptoms: bilateral lower abdominal pain, fever, vaginal discharge, cervical motion tenderness (chandelier sign). Complications: tubo-ovarian abscess, infertility (tubal damage), ectopic pregnancy, chronic pelvic pain. Fitz-Hugh-Curtis: perihepatitis (violin-string adhesions). Treatment: ceftriaxone + doxycycline ± metronidazole. Reference: DC Dutta — PID.

questions.optionB: Fallopian tubes (100% bilateral — most common site of genital TB, leading cause of tubal infertility in India)
questions.explanation: Genital TB: hematogenous spread from pulmonary/extrapulmonary TB. Most common site: fallopian tubes (100% — always bilateral). Then: endometrium (50-60%), ovaries (20-30%), cervix (5-15%). Presentation: infertility (most common), menstrual irregularities (oligomenorrhea → amenorrhea), chronic pelvic pain. Diagnosis: endometrial biopsy (granulomas — histopathological gold standard), culture (gold standard but slow), PCR (rapid), hysterosalpingography (beaded tube, lead-pipe, tobacco pouch appearance). Treatment: ATT (HRZE). Reference: DC Dutta — Genital TB.
